title: Make controls behaviorally honest
statement: A visible control must work and its pressed or selected appearance must reflect real state; otherwise remove, disable, or fix it.
rationale: False affordances and decorative state destroy trust faster than a visibly unavailable action.

prefer: Wire every visible control to real behavior.
Reflect real selected, pressed, loading, and disabled state.
avoid: Pressed styling disconnected from actual state.

when: A surface presents an interactive control or stateful action.
not_when: A clearly labeled static demonstration is intentionally non-interactive.

evidence.summary: Reported a bulk-resolution control that did nothing and looked permanently pressed.
Rejected a nonworking upvote and a navigation dropdown that opened on unintended hover.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Activated only for the owner-requested initial bootstrap because repeated independent direct instructions or corrections establish this decision boundary.

positive_examples: Wire every visible control to real behavior.
Reflect real selected, pressed, loading, and disabled state.
negative_examples: Pressed styling disconnected from actual state.

checks: Can every visible control be activated as expected?
Does visual state match application state?
Is unavailable behavior clearly disabled or removed?
